author: v5364704
text: Allow force_text and force_points to be tuples with force in x and y directions

author: v5364704
text:
Nice tool, used it when studied math.  
Because text annotations are usually longer along X axis it is sometimes more optimal to distribute them vertically with higher force. For this reason force\_text and force\_points arguments could be extended to accept both scalar and tuple with x and y forces specified separately. As far as I understand only adjust\_text(...) needs modifications when dx and dy are calculated.

author: Phlya
text: That's a good idea, thanks, I'll look into it when I have time! (Or feel free to prepare a PR).

author: Phlya
text: This is implemented in the latest commit, please check it works as you'd expect.

author: Phlya
text: Just provide a tuple with two floats/ints, for x and y for any of the three forces instead of a float/int (just a number should continue to work as it used to).

author: Phlya
text: I think it works for me, so I'll close the issue.
